revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町]]にある、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定...'
revision.text:
'''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町]]にある、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定史跡。 ==概要== 大きさは、全長約75m、高さ約4.2m、前方部幅約51m、後方部幅約12mで、周りには周溝を持つ。 [[昭和]]57年([[1982年]])、明治大学の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により、東北地方でも最大級の前方後方墳である事が判明した。そして翌年、昭和58年([[1983年]])に発掘調査が行われ、墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔広口甕型土器などを分析した結果、この古墳は、古墳時代前期後半（[[4世紀]]末）に築造されたものと推定された。主体部である被葬者の[[埋葬]]部分は未発掘である。昭和59年([[1984年]])[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 ==主な出土品== 底部穿孔広口甕型土器（ていぶせんこうひろくちかめがたどき） ==所在地== 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「川西町フレンドリープラザ」に隣接。） ==アクセス・交通== [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩で10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 ==関連項目== *北海道・東北の史跡一覧 ==参考文献== *[http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html 天神森古墳] *[http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/tenzin.html 天神森古墳] *[http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/soudan/ctrl?evt=ShowBukken&ID=06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:山形県の歴史]] [[Category:西川町]]

'''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75m、高さ約4.2m、前方部幅約51m、後方部幅約12mで、周りには周溝を持つ。 [[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）、明治大学の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により、東北地方でも最大級の前方後方墳である事が判明した。そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔広口甕型[[土器]]などを分析した結果、およそ古墳時代前期後半（[[4世紀]]末）に築造されたものと推定された。主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔広口甕型土器（ていぶせんこうひろくちかめがたどき） == 所在地 == 〒999-0121

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天神森古墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg||280px]]
天神森古墳
（写真中央、木々が生い茂る） |所属 = |所在地 = [[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]大字上小松
字天神森1430-1{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=調査要項}} |ISO = JP-06 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 全長77.5メートル
高さ4.26メートル（後方部）{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}} |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=22}} |被葬者 = |出土品 = 底部穿孔土器
広口壺形土器 |指定文化財 = 山形県指定史跡 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Yamagata |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 }} '''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=1}}、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75.58メートル、高さ約4.26メートル、後方部幅51.05メートルで{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}}、周りには周溝を持つ。 昔から「天神森の墳丘は古墳ではないか」と指摘があったが、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）5月の川西町教育委員会とまんぎり会による調査から、前方後方墳であることが確認される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=4}}。さらに[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、[[明治大学]]の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により[[東北地方]]で最大の前方後方墳である事が判明した{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=発刊によせて}}。 そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、 墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔土器・広口甕型土器と仙台[[安久東遺跡]]の遺物との比較分析から、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭ごろに築造されたものと推定される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|pp=21-22}}。 主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。 [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔土器、広口甕型土器、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、赤焼土器、片口土器{{small|（中世の[[陶器]]）}} == 所在地 == 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * [http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html 天神森古墳] * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/tenzin.html 天神森古墳] * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/soudan/ctrl?evt=ShowBukken&ID=06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * [http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ 山形県立博物館] * [http://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県の歴史]] [[Category:川西町 (山形県)]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

字天神森1430-1{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=調査要項}} |ISO = JP-06 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 全長77.5メートル
高さ4.26メートル（後方部）{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}} |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=22}} |被葬者 = |出土品 = 底部穿孔土器
広口壺形土器 |指定文化財 = 山形県指定史跡 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Yamagata |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 }} '''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=1}}、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75.58メートル、高さ約4.26メートル、後方部幅51.05メートルで{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}}、周りには周溝を持つ。 昔から「天神森の墳丘は古墳ではないか」と指摘があったが、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）5月の川西町教育委員会とまんぎり会による調査から、前方後方墳であることが確認される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=4}}。さらに[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、[[明治大学]]の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により[[東北地方]]で最大の前方後方墳である事が判明した{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=発刊によせて}}。 そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、 墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔土器・広口甕型土器と仙台[[安久東遺跡]]の遺物との比較分析から、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭ごろに築造されたものと推定される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|pp=21-22}}。 主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。 [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔土器、広口甕型土器、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、赤焼土器、片口土器{{small|（中世の[[陶器]]）}} == 所在地 == 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=天神森古墳|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html 天神森古墳] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * [http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ 山形県立博物館] * [http://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県の歴史]] [[Category:川西町 (山形県)]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天神森古墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg||280px]]
天神森古墳
（写真中央、木々が生い茂る） |所属 = |所在地 = [[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]大字上小松
字天神森1430-1{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=調査要項}} |ISO = JP-06 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 全長77.5メートル
高さ4.26メートル（後方部）{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}} |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=22}} |被葬者 = |出土品 = 底部穿孔土器
広口壺形土器 |指定文化財 = 山形県指定史跡 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Yamagata |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 }} '''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=1}}、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75.58メートル、高さ約4.26メートル、後方部幅51.05メートルで、周りには周溝を持つ。 昔から「天神森の墳丘は古墳ではないか」と指摘があったが、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）5月の川西町教育委員会とまんぎり会による調査から、前方後方墳であることが確認される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=4}}。さらに[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、[[明治大学]]の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により[[東北地方]]で最大の前方後方墳である事が判明した{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=発刊によせて}}。 そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、 墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔土器・広口甕型土器と仙台[[安久東遺跡]]の遺物との比較分析から、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭ごろに築造されたものと推定される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|pp=21-22}}。 主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。 [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔土器、広口甕型土器、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、赤焼土器、片口土器{{small|（中世の[[陶器]]）}} == 所在地 == 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北地方の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=天神森古墳|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html 天神森古墳] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=山形県立博物館 |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県の歴史]] [[Category:川西町 (山形県)]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北地方の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=天神森古墳|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html 天神森古墳] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=山形県立博物館 |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県川西町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]
[[ja:天神森古墳]]

[[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Tenjinmori Kofun | Image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | Location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | Coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == Location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinmori Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinmori Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinmori Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in 1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == Related Topics == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinmori Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|前方後方墳]] | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == *
