[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue ware]] * [[Haji ware]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}.== See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Size = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | Group = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-typed pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with artifacts from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designated as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture other_designations]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Buried Person = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | Period = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset Designation = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamagata Prefecture other_designations]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawanishi Town History]]
