Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated other_designation. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was other_designation1 as a Yamagata Prefecture other_designation on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of other_designations of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | 1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Kofun navbar}}" to "[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{citation needed|Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{citation needed|Tenjinmori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tenjinmori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kawanishi Town History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[ja:天神森古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=draft|12=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:天神森古墳 up to rev 95624557
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天神森古墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg||280px]]
天神森古墳
（写真中央、木々が生い茂る） |所属 = |所在地 = [[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]大字上小松
字天神森1430-1{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=調査要項}} |ISO = JP-06 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 全長77.5メートル
高さ4.26メートル（後方部）{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}} |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=22}} |被葬者 = |出土品 = 底部穿孔土器
広口壺形土器 |指定文化財 = 山形県指定史跡 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Yamagata |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 }} '''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=1}}、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75.58メートル、高さ約4.26メートル、後方部幅51.05メートルで、周りには周溝を持つ。 昔から「天神森の墳丘は古墳ではないか」と指摘があったが、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）5月の川西町教育委員会とまんぎり会による調査から、前方後方墳であることが確認される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=4}}。さらに[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、[[明治大学]]の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により[[東北地方]]で最大の前方後方墳である事が判明した{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=発刊によせて}}。 そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、 墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔土器・広口甕型土器と仙台[[安久東遺跡]]の遺物との比較分析から、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭ごろに築造されたものと推定される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|pp=21-22}}。 主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。 [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔土器、広口甕型土器、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、赤焼土器、片口土器{{small|（中世の[[陶器]]）}} == 所在地 == 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北地方の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=天神森古墳|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html 天神森古墳] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=山形県立博物館 |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県川西町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

字天神森1430-1{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=調査要項}} |ISO = JP-06 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 全長77.5メートル
高さ4.26メートル（後方部）{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=16}} |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=22}} |被葬者 = |出土品 = 底部穿孔土器
広口壺形土器 |指定文化財 = 山形県指定史跡 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Yamagata |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 }} '''天神森古墳'''（てんじんもりこふん）は、[[山形県]][[東置賜郡]][[川西町 (山形県)|川西町]]にある{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=1}}、[[古墳時代]]前期後半のものと推定される[[前方後方墳]]。山形県指定[[史跡]]。 == 概要 == 大きさは、全長約75.58メートル、高さ約4.26メートル、後方部幅51.05メートルで、周りには周溝を持つ。 昔から「天神森の墳丘は古墳ではないか」と指摘があったが、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）5月の川西町教育委員会とまんぎり会による調査から、前方後方墳であることが確認される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=4}}。さらに[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、[[明治大学]]の[[大塚初重]]教授の調査により[[東北地方]]で最大の前方後方墳である事が判明した{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=発刊によせて}}。 そして翌年、[[1983年]]（昭和58年）に発掘調査が行われ、 墳丘や周溝から出土した底部穿孔土器・広口甕型土器と仙台[[安久東遺跡]]の遺物との比較分析から、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭ごろに築造されたものと推定される{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|社会教育課|1982|pp=21-22}}。 主体部である被葬者の埋葬部分は未発掘である。 [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[7月17日]]には、山形県指定史跡となった。 == 主な出土品 == 底部穿孔土器、広口甕型土器、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、赤焼土器、片口土器{{small|（中世の[[陶器]]）}} == 所在地 == 〒999-0121
山形県東置賜郡川西町大字上小松　（複合文化施設「[[川西町フレンドリープラザ]]」に隣接。） == アクセス == * [[羽前小松駅]]から徒歩10分。[[タクシー]]で2分。 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北海道・東北地方の史跡一覧]] ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=天神森古墳|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html 天神森古墳] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 旅そうだん] *{{Citation | last= 社会教育課 | first= 川西町教育委員会 | author-link= 川西町 (山形県) | year= 1982 | title= 川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書第6集　山形県川西町 天神森古墳　発掘調査報告書 | periodical= | volume= | issue= | pages= | url= https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html 川西町埋蔵文化財展示資料館] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=山形県立博物館 |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ 山形大学付属博物館] {{Coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=title|name=天神森古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんもりこふん}} [[Category:山形県の古墳]] [[Category:山形県指定史跡]] [[Category:山形県川西町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|ja|川西町 (山形県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Yamagata|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|qq=}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|qq=}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|qq=}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|ja|羽前小松駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzen-Komatsu Station|qq=}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{coord|38|0|23.73|N|140|3|4.25|E|region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|qq=}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|qq=}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[Sue pottery]] * [[Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|qq=}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]][[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tenjinmori Kofun]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:山形県の古墳]]
