[[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft 1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}}

[[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}}

The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

[[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in [[:en:Kawanishi, Yamagata|Kawanishi, Yamagata]], Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Uzen-Komatsu Station|Uzen-Komatsu Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443260]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|en|Kawanishi, Yamagata|WD=Q1349146|lt=Kawanishi, Yamagata}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|en|Uzen-Komatsu Station|WD=Q7904818|lt=Uzen-Komatsu Station}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|en|Uzen-Komatsu Station|WD=Q7904818|lt=Uzen-Komatsu Station}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

[[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tenjinmori Kofun | image = [[File:Tenjinmori Kofun.jpg|280px]]
Tenjinmori Kofun
(Center of the image, covered with trees) | part_of = | location = Kawanishi Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Tenjinmori{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Survey Outline}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.00659167|N|140.05118056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | area = Length: 77.5 m
Height: 4.26 m (posterior section){{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=16}} | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=22}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Perforated-bottom pottery, wide-mouth jar pottery | Cultural Asset designation1 = Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site | type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Tenjinmori Kofun''' (天神森古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound with a square front and round back) located in {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|en|Kawanishi, Yamagata|WD=Q1349146|lt=Kawanishi, Yamagata}}, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan. It is a Yamagata Prefecture Designated Historic Site. Dating back to the late 4th to early 5th century, it is considered one of the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region. == Overview == The kofun measures approximately 77.5 meters in length, with a height of 4.26 meters and a posterior width of 51.05 meters. It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|en|Uzen-Komatsu Station|WD=Q7904818|lt=Uzen-Komatsu Station}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

It features a surrounding moat. Local speculation identified the mound as an ancient kofun for years, but it was not until a 1980 survey conducted by the Kawanishi Town Education Committee and the Mangiri Association that its classification as a Zenpō-kōhō-fun was confirmed.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=4}} In 1982, Professor {{ill|Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuaki Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}} of Meiji University identified it as the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region through further analysis.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=Preface}} An excavation conducted in 1983 revealed perforated-bottom pottery and wide-mouth jar pottery in the mound and surrounding areas. Comparisons with burial goods from the {{ill|Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja|安久東遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankyū Higashi Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Sendai helped date the kofun to the late 4th to early 5th century.{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|p=14}}{{Harvnb|Kawanishi Town Education Committee|1982|pp=21-22}} However, the burial chamber itself has not been excavated. The site was Historic Site1 as a Yamagata Prefecture Historic Site on July 17, 1984. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * Perforated-bottom pottery * Wide-mouth jar pottery * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} * Red-fired pottery * Ladle-shaped pottery {{small|(medieval ceramic wares)}} == location == The kofun is located near the {{ill|Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|ja|川西町フレンドリープラザ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Friendly Plaza|1=Q24863294|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24863294}} cultural complex at: 〒999-0121
1430-1 Oaza Kamikomatsu, Kawanishi Town, Yamagata Prefecture. == Access == * A 10-minute walk or 2-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|en|Uzen-Komatsu Station|WD=Q7904818|lt=Uzen-Komatsu Station|1=Uzen-Komatsu Station}}. == See Also == * [[List of Infobox ancient site]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Hokkaido and Tōhoku]] == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Archive.today|url=http://homepage2.nifty.com/o-obuta/kofunn/yamagata/kawanisi/tennzinnmori/tennzinnmori.html |title=Tenjinmori Kofun|date=20130427122313}} * [https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/tenjinmori-kofun.html Tenjinmori Kofun] * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/detail/06382af2170019198 Nihon Kankou] * {{Citation | last=Kawanishi Town Education Committee | year=1982 | title=Tenjinmori Kofun Excavation Report (川西町埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第6集 山形県川西町 天神森古墳) | periodical= | volume= | pages= | url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/8/8021/5932_2_%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E6%A3%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8.pdf (PDF) }} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/bunkazai/siryokan.html Kawanishi Town Archaeological Museum] * {{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~ykmuseum/ |title=Yamagata Prefectural Museum |date=20060412163103}} * [https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/museum/ Yamagata University Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|天神森古墳|version=95624557|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天神森古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11443260}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamagata prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]]
